title: Question: clocks/signsla generated in top level .cpp don't "change" in waveforms
body:
---


Author Name: **Brad Parker** (@lisper)
Original Redmine Message: 362 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I've got a top level .cpp program which controls the simulation.  It generates
multiple clocks and resets and feeds them into the top level by directly
accessing the variables, i.e. top->v__DOT__cpu__DOT__promdisable = 1;

When I do this the "change" function is never called so the waveforms never
show changes made from the .cpp file.

Is there any way I can "fix" this?  I have tried making wires inside 
the top level module and assigning the external signal but that didn't
help (seems like verilator is too smart for that trick :-)

how can I indicate a net has changed so it will change in the waveforms?
